articles.headline: Fever's Clark earns 7th technical foul; one away from 1-game ban
articles.description: Fever guard Caitlin Clark is one technical foul away from an automatic one-game suspension after she picked up her seventh of the season after jawing with Connecticut's Saniya Rivers on Wednesday night.

images.name: Caitlin Clark [600x400]
images.caption: Fever star Caitlin Clark, right, earned her seventh technical foul of the season, tying her with Atlanta's Angel Reese for the league high.

images.name: Caitlin Clark [600x400]
images.credit: Photo by Pepper Robinson/NBAE via Getty Images

images.name: Caitlin Clark and Saniya Rivers issued double techs for jawing at each other
images.caption: Caitlin Clark and Saniya Rivers issued double techs for jawing at each other

articles.headline: Shepard has a triple-double and the Wings hold off the Fire 101-97
articles.description: — Jessica Shepard had 18 points, 15 rebounds and 10 assists, and the Dallas Wings withstood a late rally for a 101-97 victory over the Portland Fire on Wednesday night.

images.name: Jessica Shepard collects a triple-double vs. the Fire
images.caption: Jessica Shepard collects a triple-double vs. the Fire

articles.headline: Who has the most triple-doubles in WNBA history? Key stats
articles.description: A triple-double is an impressive feat. Which WNBA players have achieved it? Check out a historical rundown of stat leaders.

images.caption: Alyssa Thomas holds the WNBA record for career triple-doubles.

articles.headline: Fire guard Barker helped off floor with leg injury vs. Wings
articles.description: Fire guard Sarah Ashlee Barker tumbled out of bounds after making a pass and had to be helped off the floor with a left leg injury early in Wednesday's game against the Wings.

images.name: Sarah Ashlee Barker [600x400]
images.credit: Photo by Jaden Coleman/NBAE via Getty Images

articles.headline: Caitlin Clark scores 27 and the Fever set a franchise scoring record in a 123-88 win over the Sun
articles.description: — Caitlin Clark had 27 points and 11 assists, Kelsey Mitchell added 23 points and the Indiana Fever set a franchise record for points in a game with their 123-88 victory over the Connecticut Sun on Wednesday night.

articles.headline: Austin scores 25 and has go-ahead three-point play as Mystics shock Aces 100-99
articles.description: — Shakira Austin scored 25 points, converting a three-point play after an offensive rebound with 12.8 seconds left, to lead the Washington Mystics to a 100-99 victory over the Las Vegas Aces on Wednesday night.

articles.headline: Ionescu scores 29 points, 9 in the last minute, to rally the Liberty to a 95-94 win over the Sky
articles.description: — Sabrina Ionescu scored nine of her 29 points in the final minute, Jonquel Jones made the tiebreaking free throw with 3.4 seconds left and the New York Liberty rallied to beat the Chicago Sky 95-94 on Wednesday night.

articles.headline: Delle Donne among legends in WNBA's Shooting Stars event
articles.description: Two-time MVP Elena Delle Donne will be among four WNBA legends who will team with a current WNBA player and a player from the Nike EYBL league for the Shooting Stars event Friday night as part of All-Star Weekend.

images.name: Elena Delle Donne [600x400]
images.caption: It's unclear whether Elena Delle Donne, who sat out the 2024 season, wants to continue playing or if she's physically capable of doing so. And if the Valkyries pick her, other teams could trade for the two-time MVP.

articles.headline: Lynx star Napheesa Collier returns with 20-point double-double
articles.description: Napheesa Collier made her return Wednesday with 24 points and 10 rebounds in 22 minutes in the Lynx's win over the Storm.

images.name: Napheesa Collier scores 24 vs. Seattle Storm
images.caption: Napheesa Collier scores 24 vs. Seattle Storm

articles.headline: WNBA, WNBPA meeting addressed 'abusive messages' to players
articles.description: The WNBA and players' association held a videoconference Tuesday to discuss a variety of issues, including recent "racist, hateful, and abusive messages directed at players," according to a joint statement released Wednesday.

article.headline: A’ja Wilson and Jackie Young combine for 50 points to lead the Aces past the Tempo 109-83
article.description: — A’ja Wilson scored 26 points, Jackie Young had a season-high 24 points to go with 12 assists, and the Las Vegas Aces beat the Toronto Tempo 109-83 on Monday night.

article.story: TORONTO -- — A’ja Wilson scored 26 points, Jackie Young had a season-high 24 points to go with 12 assists, and the Las Vegas Aces beat the Toronto Tempo 109-83 on Monday night. Wilson went 11 of 14 from the field and Young made four 3-pointers, the last to give Las Vegas a 99-79 lead with 3:39 left in the fourth. Toronto was without coach Sandy Brondello, who was serving a one-game suspension for an “inappropriate” comment she made about Atlanta star Angel Reese during the Dream’s 111-92 win on Friday. Associate head coach Olaf Lange led the team in Brondello’s absence. NaLyssa Smith chipped in with 18 points for Las Vegas (18-7). Becky Hammon passed Richie Adubato and Pokey Chatman (134) into 13th place in WNBA history for career coaching wins. Isabelle Harrison had a career-high 26 points to go with 11 rebounds for Toronto (10-16), which has lost three straight games and seven of its last eight. María Conde had 15 points and eight rebounds, while Marina Mabrey added 14 points. The Tempo were also without Brittney Sykes (foot), Kiki Rice (ankle) and Nyara Sabally (rib cage). Up next Aces: At Washington on Wednesday. Tempo: Play at Minnesota on July 28, following the All-Star break. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
